description_long: This is a tall, vertical utility pole constructed primarily from weathered wood or concrete, featuring two horizontal crossarms near the top. Each crossarm supports a series of insulators, which are spaced evenly and appear to be made of ceramic or composite material. The pole is devoid of any visible transformers, streetlights, or identification tags, and its surface shows a rough, utilitarian finish typical of outdoor infrastructure. The crossarms are stacked one above the other, both oriented horizontally and parallel to each other, with insulators mounted on top. The overall color palette is dominated by gray tones, with subtle hints of rust or brown on the insulator mounts. This structure is designed to support electrical or telecommunication wires in an urban or suburban environment.
description: A tall, gray utility pole with two stacked horizontal crossarms, each supporting multiple insulators, and a rough, weathered surface.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the full height of the pole, both horizontal crossarms, and all insulators clearly aligned.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the thinness of the crossarms and the pole, with insulators barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying both crossarms and insulators, but with less detail on the mounts.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the narrow silhouette of the pole and crossarms.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: Two horizontal crossarms are stacked vertically near the top of the pole, parallel to each other.
